# LLMOD Copilot Instructions
- Stack: .NET 8 WPF desktop app with CommunityToolkit.Mvvm (Source Generators for `[ObservableProperty]` and `[RelayCommand]`). No tests.
- Entry point: [MainWindow.xaml](MainWindow.xaml) is a thin 70px-wide always-on-top tray that positions itself on load; it opens other windows via click handlers in [MainWindow.xaml.cs](MainWindow.xaml.cs).
- Windows: [Views/ChatWindow.xaml](Views/ChatWindow.xaml) (full-height right side chat), [Views/SystemMonitorWindow.xaml](Views/SystemMonitorWindow.xaml) (main telemetry UI), [Views/SystemMonitorDock.xaml](Views/SystemMonitorDock.xaml) (bottom-left dock, can toggle monitor view), [Views/ProcessConfigWindow.xaml](Views/ProcessConfigWindow.xaml) (placeholder priority config). Each window positions itself manually using `SystemParameters`.
- DataContexts are set in XAML: Chat uses `LLMOD.SystemMonitorViewModels.ChatViewModel`; System monitor uses `LLMOD.ViewModels.SystemMonitorViewModel`. Avoid wiring UI to the stub partial in [LLMOD/SystemMonitorViewModels/SystemMonitorViewModel.cs](LLMOD/SystemMonitorViewModels/SystemMonitorViewModel.cs); the real logic lives in [ViewModels/SystemMonitorViewModel.cs](ViewModels/SystemMonitorViewModel.cs).
- Resources: [App.xaml](App.xaml) defines global brushes and styles plus converters keyed `BoolToColorConverter`, `TrendConverter`, `InverseTrayConverter`. Ensure these resource keys stay intact when adding views.
- Converters live in [Helpers/Converters.cs](Helpers/Converters.cs); they rely on the `MonitorView` enum from `LLMOD.ViewModels`. Fix/align namespaces if adding new converters to avoid the existing mixed `LLMOD.SystemMonitorViewModel(s)` imports.
- MVVM pattern: ViewModels are partial classes with toolkit attributes generating backing members (e.g., `SendMessageCommand`, `SetPerfModeCommand`). Add properties/commands via attributes to keep binding names consistent.
- System monitor simulation: [ViewModels/SystemMonitorViewModel.cs](ViewModels/SystemMonitorViewModel.cs) seeds example telemetry (CPU/GPU/RAM/network) and Kaia service statuses; a 1s `Timer` mutates values. `ToggleViewCommand` is a stub—extend here if implementing view modes.
- Chat flow: [SystemMonitorViewModels/ChatViewModel.cs](ViewModels/ChatViewModel.cs) starts with a system message and echoes user input with a "Processed:" prefix. `SendMessageCommand` clears input and appends messages.
- UI components: [Controls/NestedGauge.xaml](Controls/NestedGauge.xaml) is a static visual gauge (no dependency properties). Use bindings only where already declared.
- Window chrome/click-through: [Helpers/WindowHelper.cs](Helpers/WindowHelper.cs) sets `WS_EX_TRANSPARENT`/`WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW`; [ChatWindow.xaml.cs](Views/ChatWindow.xaml.cs) calls it to control click-through. Keep handles via `WindowInteropHelper` when adding similar behavior.
- Styling: Accent colors and border effects live in [App.xaml](App.xaml). Buttons are restyled globally; reuse existing styles (`LLMOD_Border`, `LLMOD_ScrollViewer`) to match the cyberpunk look.
- Performance/priority controls: `PerfMode` and `PriorityMode` enums and commands are defined in [ViewModels/SystemMonitorViewModel.cs](ViewModels/SystemMonitorViewModel.cs); UI bindings expect these exact names/values.
- Build/run: standard `dotnet build` on [LLMOD.csproj](LLMOD.csproj) (WPF, `UseWPF` true, `AllowUnsafeBlocks` enabled). Base output path is overridden to `bin` under the repo. Use VS/VS Code with .NET SDK 8 installed; no special tasks required.
- Assets: icon at [Assets/LLMOD60x60.ico](Assets/LLMOD60x60.ico) is copied to output; keep resource includes if replacing.
- Gotchas: `SystemMonitorViewModels` namespace is used for chat but the monitor VM is under `LLMOD.ViewModels`; watch namespace typos when creating bindings. `ToggleViewCommand` is unimplemented—calling it currently no-ops. `ChatWindow` hides on deactivate; show it manually when re-opening from the tray.
- When adding UI, keep transparency/topmost expectations (most windows set `AllowsTransparency=True`, `Topmost=True`, `WindowStyle=None`). Positioning math assumes a single primary screen.
- Keep instructions concise inside XAML comments: several comments mark "CRITICAL" lines—preserve those imports to avoid missing types.
